description_long: This sculpture is a detailed stone bust depicting the head of a serene figure, likely inspired by traditional representations of the Buddha. The head features a prominent ushnisha (cranial protuberance) with tiered, rounded forms, and intricate hair curls carved in a symmetrical pattern. The face is calm and meditative, with closed eyes, arched eyebrows, and full lips, exuding a sense of tranquility. Large, elongated earlobes extend downward, a common feature in Buddhist iconography. The sculpture sits on a simple rectangular base, which appears to be made of a different, darker material. The surface of the sculpture is matte and slightly rough, with a uniform light brown stone coloration.
description: A serene stone bust of a meditative figure with intricate hair curls, elongated earlobes, and a calm facial expression, mounted on a dark rectangular base.
description_view_0: Front view showing the calm face, closed eyes, and symmetrical features of the bust.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the profile, elongated earlobe, and detailed hair curls.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the patterned hair and the back of the head and base.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the profile, elongated earlobe, and the layered ushnisha.
attribute_location_context: No visible context; background is isolated and does not indicate a specific setting.
